post.summary: Registers a new user account using the specified registration details.
post.description:
This method creates a new user account with the provided credentials. The
            registration may fail if the user name or email address is already in use, or if the password does not meet
            the required criteria. The response does not include detailed error information; for more granular feedback,
            consider extending the response model.

requestBody.description:
An object containing the user registration information, including user name, email address, and password.
            Cannot be null.

post.summary: Books seats for an event based on the provided booking request.
post.description:
This method processes the booking asynchronously and returns appropriate HTTP status
            codes based on the outcome of the booking request.

requestBody.description:
The event booking request containing details such as event ID and number of seats to book. This parameter
            cannot be null.

post.summary: Books seats for an event based on the provided booking request.
post.description:
This method processes the booking asynchronously and returns appropriate HTTP status
            codes based on the outcome of the booking request.

requestBody.description:
The event booking request containing details such as event ID and number of seats to book. This parameter
            cannot be null.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of active event categories.
Searches for client contacts using the specified phone number or email address.
get.description:
If both phone and email are null or empty, the request returns a BadRequest response
            indicating that at least one search parameter must be specified.

parameters.name: phoneRegionCodeId
parameters.description: The country code associated with the phone number. This parameter is optional but should be provided if phoneNumber is used for searching.

parameters.name: phoneNumber
parameters.description: The phone number to search for. At least one of the parameters, either phone or email, must be provided.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email address to search for. At least one of the parameters, either phone or email, must be provided.

get.summary: Asynchronously retrieves a paginated list of clients that match the specified query parameters.
get.description:
Use this method to obtain a subset of clients based on filtering and pagination
            options. Ensure that the query parameters are set appropriately to retrieve the desired results.

post.summary: Creates a new client using the specified request data.
post.description:
This method is asynchronous and processes the client creation request using the
            injected client service. Ensure that the request data is valid to avoid errors during processing.

requestBody.description:
The request object containing the information required to create a new client. This parameter must not be
            null.

get.summary: Retrieves the details of a client specified by the unique client identifier.
get.description:
This method asynchronously obtains client details from the client service. Ensure
            that the provided client ID is valid and exists in the system.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the client to retrieve. Must be a positive long value.

put.summary: Updates the client information for the specified client identifier with the provided data.
put.description:
This method performs an asynchronous update operation. If the update fails, an error
            message is returned indicating that the client could not be updated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the client to update. Must be a valid long integer.

requestBody.description: An object containing the updated client information. Cannot be null.

delete.summary: Deletes the client with the specified identifier.
delete.description:
This method is asynchronous. If the specified client does not exist, a 404 Not Found
            response is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the client to delete. Must be a positive long value.

post.summary: Generates and returns a CSV file containing client information for the specified client IDs.
post.description:
Use this endpoint to export client records as a CSV file for the provided client IDs.
            The response includes a UTF-8 encoded CSV file named 'clients.csv'. Ensure that the client IDs correspond to
            existing clients to receive data.

requestBody.description: A list of client IDs for which to retrieve and export client data. Cannot be null or empty.

get.summary:
Retrieves a list of available credit status options, each represented by an integer value and a localized
label.
get.description:
The returned labels are localized according to the current culture, enabling
            appropriate display in different languages. The list is generated from the Odasoft.XBOL.Commons.Enums.CreditStatus
            enumeration, ensuring that all defined statuses are included.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of available client types, each represented by an integer value and a localized label.
get.description:
The labels for each client type are localized based on the current culture using the
            application's localization resources. This method is intended to provide user-friendly, culture-aware
            options for client type selection in user interfaces.

get.summary:
Retrieves a list of available payment frequency options, each represented by an integer value and a
localized label.
get.description:
The labels for each payment frequency are localized according to the current culture,
            ensuring that the returned text is appropriate for the user's language settings. The list is generated from
            the PaymentFrequency enumeration.

get.summary: Retrieves the client credit account information using the client Id as identifier.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: Client unique identifier.

put.summary: Updates an exisiting client credit account with the specified details

parameters.name: creditAccountId
parameters.description: Unique identifier.

requestBody.description: An object containing the updated credit account information.

parameters.name: creditAccountId
parameters.description: Unique identifier

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of credit orders that match the specified query parameters.
get.description:
This method is asynchronous and returns an HTTP 200 response with the paginated
            results if successful. Ensure that the provided query parameters are valid to avoid unexpected
            results.

get.summary: Retrieves a paged list of credit transactions that match the specified query parameters.
get.description:
This method is asynchronous and may take additional time to complete depending on the
            number of transactions and the complexity of the query parameters.

post.summary: Creates a new credit transaction for the credit account based on the specified request data.

parameters.name: creditAccountId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the credit account.

requestBody.description: An object containing the info for the credit transaction to create.

put.summary: Updates an existing credit transaction with the specified details.

parameters.name: creditTransactionId
parameters.description: Unique identifier.

requestBody.description: The request object containing the updated credit transaction information.

parameters.name: creditTransactionId
parameters.description: Unique identifier

get.summary: Retrieves a list of the event's images.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the event.

post.summary: Creates a new event image with the specified info in each request.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the Event.

order.description: Order of the image when display.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of the event`s images filtered by image type.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the event.

parameters.name: imageType
parameters.description: The ImageType of the images wanted.

put.summary: Updates an exisiting event image with the specified details.

parameters.name: eventImageId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the Event.

order.description: Order of the image when display.

delete.summary: Deletes and existing event image with the specified Id.

parameters.name: eventImageId
parameters.description: The unique Event Image Id.

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of events that match the specified filter and sorting criteria.

parameters.name: venues
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of venue identifiers used to filter events by their associated venues. If null or
            empty, events from all venues are included.

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of category identifiers used to filter events by their associated categories. If null
            or empty, events from all categories are included.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description:
The start date and time for filtering events. Only events occurring on or after this date are included. If
            null, no lower date bound is applied.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description:
The end date and time for filtering events. Only events occurring on or before this date are included. If
            null, no upper date bound is applied.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description:
A search term used to filter events by their title or description. If null or empty, no search filtering is
            applied.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description:
The property name by which to sort the events. Valid options: 'name', 'category', 'venue', 'createdat'.
            Defaults to schedule start date if null or unrecognized.

parameters.name: descending
parameters.description:
Indicates whether the sorting should be in descending order. If true, results are sorted in descending
            order; otherwise, ascending order is used. If null, the default sort direction is applied.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
The page number of results to retrieve. Must be a positive integer if specified. If null, the first page is
            returned.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The number of events to include per page. Must be a positive integer if specified. If null, a default page
            size is used.

parameters.name: seasonId
parameters.description: The identifier of the season to filter events by. If null, events from all seasons are included.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
The status value used to filter events (such as Active, Cancelled, etc.). If null, events of all statuses
            are included.

parameters.name: upcoming
parameters.description: Indicates whether to filter for events that have an schedule currently on sale. If null, all events are included

post.summary: Creates a new event using the specified request data and returns the result of the operation.
post.description:
If the model state is invalid, the method returns a 400 Bad Request response. On
            successful creation, a 201 Created response is returned with the location of the newly created event. If
            the creation fails, a 422 Unprocessable Entity response is returned.

requestBody.description:
The request object containing the details required to create a new event. Must not be null and must satisfy
            model validation requirements.

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of events that are currently available for sale that match the specified filter and sorting criteria.

parameters.name: venues
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of venue identifiers used to filter events by their associated venues. If null or
            empty, events from all venues are included.

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of category identifiers used to filter events by their associated categories. If null
            or empty, events from all categories are included.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description:
The start date and time for filtering events. Only events occurring on or after this date are included. If
            null, no lower date bound is applied.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description:
The end date and time for filtering events. Only events occurring on or before this date are included. If
            null, no upper date bound is applied.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description:
A search term used to filter events by their title or description. If null or empty, no search filtering is
            applied.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description:
The property name by which to sort the events. Valid options: 'name', 'category', 'venue'.
            Defaults to schedule start date if null or unrecognized.

parameters.name: descending
parameters.description:
Indicates whether the sorting should be in descending order. If true, results are sorted in descending
            order; otherwise, ascending order is used. If null, the default sort direction is applied.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
The page number of results to retrieve. Must be a positive integer if specified. If null, the first page is
            returned.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The number of events to include per page. Must be a positive integer if specified. If null, a default page
            size is used.

get.summary: Retrieves the event details for the specified event identifier.
get.description:
This method asynchronously fetches event information from the event service. If the
            event is not found, a 404 response is returned.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the event to retrieve. Must be a positive long value.

get.summary: Retrieves the event catalog as a collection of list items.

put.summary: Updates the details of an existing event identified by its unique identifier.
put.description:
The method validates the input model state before attempting to update the event. If
            the model state is invalid, a BadRequest response is returned. If the update cannot be processed, an
            UnprocessableEntity response is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the event to update. Must be a positive long value.

requestBody.description: An object containing the updated event details. This parameter is required and cannot be null.

delete.summary: Deletes the event identified by the specified unique identifier.
delete.description:
This method does not delete a event if the specified identifier does not match any
            existing event.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the event to delete. Must be a valid long integer corresponding to an existing
            event.

get.summary: Retrieves the event schedule with the specified identifier.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the event schedule to retrieve.

post.summary: Creates a new event schedule using the specified request data and returns the result of the operation.
post.description:
If the model state is invalid, the method returns a 400 Bad Request response. On
            successful creation, a 201 Created response is returned with the location of the newly created event schedule. If
            the creation fails, a 422 Unprocessable Entity response is returned.

requestBody.description:
The request object containing the details required to create a new event schedule. Must not be null and must satisfy
            model validation requirements.

put.summary: Updates the details of an existing event schedule identified by its unique identifier.
put.description:
The method validates the input model state before attempting to update the event schedule. If
            the model state is invalid, a BadRequest response is returned. If the update cannot be processed, an
            UnprocessableEntity response is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the event schedule to update. Must be a positive long value.

requestBody.description: An object containing the updated event schedule details. This parameter is required and cannot be null.

delete.summary: Deletes the event schedule identified by the specified unique identifier.
delete.description:
This method does not delete a event schedule if the specified identifier does not match any
            existing event schedule.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the event schedule to delete. Must be a valid long integer corresponding to an existing
            event schedule.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of seat prices for the specified event.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the event for which to retrieve seat prices.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of zone prices for the specified event.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the event for which to retrieve zone prices.

post.summary: Initiates a request to hold the specified seats for an event and returns a token representing the seat hold.
post.description:
This method is asynchronous and may involve network communication with the ticketing
            service. Ensure that the request contains valid seat information to avoid errors.

requestBody.description:
An object containing details about the seats to be held, including event and seat identifiers. Must not be
            null.

get.summary: Retrieves a hold token associated with the specified token string.
get.description:
This method asynchronously obtains a hold token from the ticketing client. Ensure
            that the holdToken parameter is valid to prevent errors.

parameters.name: holdToken
parameters.description: The hold token string used to identify the specific hold request. This parameter cannot be null or empty.

delete.summary: Releases the seats held by the specified hold token.
delete.description:
This asynchronous operation releases all seats associated with the provided hold
            token. Ensure that the hold token is valid and corresponds to an active hold before calling this
            method.

parameters.name: holdToken
parameters.description: The token that identifies the held seats to be released. This value cannot be null or empty.

get.summary: Retreives the detail of a legal representative using the client Id as the identifier.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the client.

delete.summary: Deletes the legal representative with the specified client as identifier.

post.summary: Creates a new legal representative based on the specified request data.

requestBody.description: The details of the legal representative to create.

put.summary: Updates an existing legal representative with the specified details, using the Client Id as unique identifier.

requestBody.description: The request object containing the updated legal representative information.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of the reference's media.

parameters.name: referenceId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the reference.

parameters.name: referenceType
parameters.description: The AdminSaleType of the reference.

parameters.name: referenceId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the reference.

parameters.name: referenceType
parameters.description: The AdminSaleType of the reference.

parameters.name: mediaType
parameters.description: The MediaType of the media wanted.

parameters.description: The unique Id of the reference.

parameters.name: referenceType
parameters.description: The AdminSaleType of the reference.

parameters.name: mediaType
parameters.description: The type of the media.

order.description: Order of the media when display.

put.summary: Updates an exisiting media with the specified details.

order.description: Order of the media when display.

delete.summary: Deletes an existing media with the specified Id.

post.summary: Performs the specified action on the order identified by the given order ID.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the order on which to perform the action.

requestBody.description: An object containing the details of the action to perform on the order. Cannot be null.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of available actions and logs associated with the specified order.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the order for which to retrieve actions and logs.

post.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of orders that match the specified filter criteria.
post.description:
This method is asynchronous and should be awaited. Ensure that the filter criteria
            are valid to avoid unexpected results. The returned list may be empty if no orders match the specified
            filters.

requestBody.description:
An object containing the filter criteria to apply when retrieving the order list, such as date range, order
            status, and customer identifier. Cannot be null.

get.summary: Retrieves the season event information associated with the specified order reference for a client.
get.description:
This method is intended to provide information about the season pass linked to the
            client's order reference. It is recommended to refactor this method to retrieve season pass information
            using client ID and season ID to reduce dependency on the order reference.

parameters.name: orderReference
parameters.description:
The unique identifier for the order reference used to fetch the corresponding season event information. This
            parameter cannot be null or empty.

get.summary: Retrieves the information of the order to be renewed and to display the information that order.

parameters.name: orderReference
parameters.description: The unique reference identifier of the order.

get.summary: Retrieves detailed information about an order using the specified order reference.

parameters.name: orderReference
parameters.description: The unique reference identifier of the order to retrieve information for. Cannot be null or empty.

get.summary: Evaluates whether a specific order is eligible for renewal.

parameters.name: orderReference
parameters.description: The unique reference identifier of the order.

get.summary: Gets a list of phone region codes, including their corresponding dial codes and flag emojis.
Retrieves the prices for the specified schedule identifier.
get.description:
This method performs an asynchronous lookup for the schedule prices. If no schedule prices are
            found for the given identifier, the response will be 404 Not Found.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the schedule prices to retrieve. Must be a positive long value.

parameters.name: venueMapId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the venue map to retrieve. Must be a positive long value.

post.summary: Creates prices for an entity using the specified request data and returns the result of the operation.
post.description:
If the model state is invalid, the method returns a 400 Bad Request response. On
            successful creation, a 201 Created response is returned with the location of the newly created prices. If
            the creation fails, a 422 Unprocessable Entity response is returned.

requestBody.description:
The request object containing the details required to create new prices. Must not be null and must satisfy
            model validation requirements.

put.summary: Updates the existing prices of an entity identified by their unique identifier.
put.description:
The method validates the input model state before attempting to update the prices. If
            the model state is invalid, a BadRequest response is returned. If the update cannot be processed, an
            UnprocessableEntity response is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the prices to update. Must be a positive long value.

requestBody.description: An object containing the updated prices details. This parameter is required and cannot be null.

post.summary: Generates and returns a CSV file containing prices information for the specified schedule and venue map.
post.description:
Use this endpoint to export prices records as a CSV file for the provided schedule and venue map IDs.
            The response includes a UTF-8 encoded CSV file named 'prices.csv'. Ensure that the schedule and venue map IDs correspond to
            existing records to receive data.

requestBody.description: An object containing the IDs of the schedule and venue map for which to retrieve and export prices data. Must be a valid PricesCsvRequest object.

get.summary: Retrieves a summary report of seat availability grouped by section.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The event key to generate the report for.

get.summary: Retrieves a summary report of seat availability grouped by zone.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The event key to generate the report for.

get.summary: Retrieves a summary report of seat availability grouped by availability (available vs not_available).

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The event key to generate the report for.

get.summary:
Retrieves a summary report of seat availability grouped by availability reason
(available, booked, reservedByToken, not_for_sale, and any custom statuses).

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The event key to generate the report for.

get.summary:
Retrieves a list of available season pass statuses, each represented by its enumeration value and a
localized label.
get.description:
The labels for each status are localized according to the current culture. Ensure
            that localization resources for season pass statuses are properly configured to provide accurate
            labels.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of reasons for which a season pass may be suspended.
get.description:
The method uses the SeasonPassSuspendedReason enumeration to generate
            the list of reasons. The labels are localized based on the current culture.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of available season pass renewal types, each represented by an enumeration item.
get.description:
This method uses localization to provide user-friendly labels for each renewal type
            based on the current culture.

put.summary: Updates the status of a season pass identified by the specified ID.
put.description:
If the provided ID does not match the ID in the request body, a 400 Bad Request
            response is returned. If the season pass is not found, a 404 Not Found response is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the season pass to update. This value must match the ID provided in the request
            body.

requestBody.description:
An object containing the new status information for the season pass. This object must include a valid
            SeasonPassId that corresponds to the ID in the route.

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of seasons that match the specified query parameters.
get.description:
This method is asynchronous and may take additional time to complete depending on the
            data source and query complexity. Ensure that the provided query parameters are valid to avoid unexpected
            results.

post.summary: Creates a new season using the specified request data and returns the result of the operation.
post.description:
If the model state is invalid, the method returns a 400 Bad Request response. On
            successful creation, a 201 Created response is returned with the location of the newly created season. If
            the creation fails, a 422 Unprocessable Entity response is returned.

requestBody.description:
The request object containing the details required to create a new season. Must not be null and must satisfy
            model validation requirements.

get.summary: Retrieves the details of a season specified by its unique identifier.
get.description:
This method performs an asynchronous lookup for the season using the provided
            identifier. If no season exists for the given identifier, the response will indicate that the resource was
            not found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the season to retrieve. Must be a positive long value.

put.summary: Updates the details of an existing season identified by its unique identifier.
put.description:
The method validates the input model state before attempting to update the season. If
            the model state is invalid, a BadRequest response is returned. If the update cannot be processed, an
            UnprocessableEntity response is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the season to update. Must be a positive long value.

requestBody.description: An object containing the updated season details. This parameter is required and cannot be null.

delete.summary: Deletes the season identified by the specified unique identifier.
delete.description:
This method does not delete a season if the specified identifier does not match any
            existing season.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the season to delete. Must be a valid long integer corresponding to an existing
            season.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of seat prices for the specified season.
get.description:
This method performs an asynchronous operation to fetch seat prices associated with
            the provided season ID. The returned list reflects the current pricing for all seats in the specified
            season.

parameters.name: seasonId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the season for which to obtain seat prices. Must be a positive long value.

get.summary: Retrieves the collection of zone prices associated with the specified season identifier.
get.description:
This method is asynchronous and responds to an HTTP GET request. Ensure that the
            provided season identifier exists to avoid receiving an empty result.

parameters.name: seasonId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the season for which to obtain zone prices. Must be a valid long integer.

get.summary: Retrieves the seat management detail for a bookable unit identified by its Seats.io external key.
get.description:
Resolves the external key against event schedules first, then seasons.
            Returns the data needed to render the seat selector/availability page.

parameters.name: externalKey
parameters.description: The Seats.io external key identifying the event schedule or season.

post.summary:
Blocks the specified seats, setting reason and color metadata in Seats.io
and marking them as not for sale in the local database.

parameters.name: externalKey
parameters.description: The Seats.io external key identifying the event schedule or season.

requestBody.description: The seat keys to block along with the reason and color for visual identification.

put.summary: Updates the reason and color metadata of already-blocked seats without changing their for-sale status.

parameters.name: externalKey
parameters.description: The Seats.io external key identifying the event schedule or season.

requestBody.description: The seat keys along with the updated reason and color.

post.summary:
Unblocks the specified seats, making them available for sale again
and clearing their metadata from Seats.io.

parameters.name: externalKey
parameters.description: The Seats.io external key identifying the event schedule or season.

requestBody.description: The seat keys to unblock.

post.summary: Holds the specified seats by creating a temporary hold token.

parameters.name: externalKey
parameters.description: The Seats.io external key identifying the event schedule or season.

requestBody.description: The seat labels to hold.

post.summary: Releases the specified seats, resetting their status to free.

parameters.name: externalKey
parameters.description: The Seats.io external key identifying the event schedule or season.

requestBody.description: The seat labels to release, with an optional hold token to scope the release.

get.summary: Retrieves the hold token details for the specified token string.

parameters.name: holdToken
parameters.description: The hold token string to look up.

delete.summary: Expires the specified hold token, releasing all seats held by it.

parameters.name: holdToken
parameters.description: The hold token to expire.

post.summary:
Books the specified seats, optionally consuming a hold token,
and creates the corresponding Order in the database.

parameters.name: externalKey
parameters.description: The Seats.io external key identifying the event schedule or season.

requestBody.description: The seats with prices, optional hold token, and booking details.

get.summary: Retrieves the client credit settings.
Retrieves a list of suite agreements.

post.summary: Creates a new Suite Agreement using the specified request data.

requestBody.description: The details of the Suite Agreement to create. Cannot be null.

put.summary: Updates an existing suite agreement with the specified details.

requestBody.description: The request object containing the updated suite agreement information. Must not be null.

get.summary: Retrieves the suite agreement with the specified identifier.

parameters.name: suiteAgreementId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite agreement to retrieve.

delete.summary: Deletes the suite agreement with the specified identifier.

parameters.name: suiteAgreementId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite agreement to delete.

post.summary: Uploads the file associated with the specified suite agreement.

suiteAgreementId.description: The unique identifier of the suite agreement whose file is to be downloaded.

get.summary: Downloads the file associated with the specified suite agreement.

parameters.name: suiteAgreementId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite agreement whose file is to be downloaded.

get.summary: Downloads multiple suite agreement files as a single ZIP archive.
get.description:
The ZIP archive will contain one entry for each suite agreement file found. The file
            name of the ZIP will include a timestamp to ensure uniqueness. If any of the specified IDs do not correspond
            to existing files, only the files that are found will be included in the archive.

parameters.name: suiteAgreementIds
parameters.description: A list of suite agreement IDs for which to retrieve and download files. Must not be null or empty.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of amenities associated with the specified suite.

parameters.name: suiteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite for which to retrieve amenities.

post.summary: Saves the list of amenity identifiers associated with the specified suite.
post.description:
This action replaces any existing amenities for the suite with the provided list. The
            operation is idempotent; repeated calls with the same parameters yield the same result.

parameters.name: suiteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite for which amenities are being set.

requestBody.description: A list of amenity identifiers to associate with the suite. The list may be empty to remove all amenities.

get.summary: Retrieves the catalog of available amenities for suites.
Retrieves the collection of images associated with the specified suite.

parameters.name: suiteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite for which to retrieve images.

post.summary: Uploads an image for the specified suite and returns the result of the operation.

parameters.name: suiteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite to which the image will be associated.

imageType.description: The type of image being uploaded. Specifies how the image will be used or displayed.

order.description: The display order of the image within the suite. Must be a non-negative integer.

file.description: The image file to upload. Must be a valid, non-null file provided in the form data.

get.summary: Retrieves the collection of images associated with a specific suite and filtered by image type.

parameters.name: suiteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite for which to retrieve images.

parameters.name: imageType
parameters.description: The type of images to retrieve for the specified suite.

put.summary: Updates the image associated with a suite by its unique identifier.

parameters.name: suiteImageId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite image to update.

imageType.description: The type of the image to associate with the suite.

order.description: The display order of the image within the suite. Must be a non-negative integer.

file.description: The image file to upload and associate with the suite. Cannot be null.

delete.summary: Deletes the suite image with the specified identifier.
delete.description:
This action does not return content on successful deletion. If the suite image cannot
            be deleted, an error message is provided in the response body.

parameters.name: suiteImageId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite image to delete.

get.summary: Retrieves the suite-level catalog as a list of items.
Retrieves the suite level catalog for the specified venue.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the venue for which to retrieve the suite level catalog.

post.summary: Creates a new suite level using the specified request data.
post.description:
Returns a 400 Bad Request response if the request model is invalid, or a 422
            Unprocessable Entity response if the suite level could not be created.

requestBody.description: The request object containing the details required to create the suite level. Cannot be null.

put.summary: Updates the suite level with the specified details.
put.description:
Returns a 400 Bad Request response if the request model is invalid, or a 422
            Unprocessable Entity response if the update operation fails.

requestBody.description:
An object containing the updated suite level information. Must not be null and must satisfy all model
            validation requirements.

delete.summary: Deletes the suite level with the specified identifier.

parameters.name: suiteLevelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite level to delete.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of suites available for the specified venue.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the venue for which to retrieve suites.

get.summary: Retrieves the details of a suite with the specified identifier.

parameters.name: suiteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite to retrieve.

put.summary: Updates an existing suite with the specified details.

parameters.name: suiteId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the Suite.

requestBody.description:
The request object containing the updated suite information. Must not be null and must satisfy all
            validation requirements.

delete.summary: Deletes the suite with the specified identifier.

parameters.name: suiteId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite to delete.

post.summary: Creates a new suite based on the specified request data.

requestBody.description: The details of the suite to create. Must not be null and must satisfy all model validation requirements.

get.summary: Retrieves the catalog of suite items associated with the specified suite level.

parameters.name: suiteLevelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the suite level for which to retrieve the catalog.

post.summary: Enqueues a test email via Hangfire.
Enqueues an order confirmation email via Hangfire using real order data.

get.summary: Renders the order confirmation email template as HTML for design iteration.
Renders the support email template as HTML for design iteration based on the order's action.
Retrieves the list of amenities available for the specified venue.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the venue for which to retrieve amenities.

post.summary: Saves the list of amenity identifiers associated with the specified venue.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the venue for which amenities are being set.

requestBody.description: A list of amenity identifiers to associate with the venue. The list may be empty to remove all amenities.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of amenities for use in selection lists or dropdowns.
Retrieves a list of the venue's images.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the venue.

post.summary: Creates a new venue image with the specified info in each request.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the Venue.

order.description: Order of the image when display.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of the venue`s images filtered by image type.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the venue.

parameters.name: imageType
parameters.description: The ImageType of the images wanted.

put.summary: Updates an exisiting venue image with the specified details.

parameters.name: venueImageId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the Venue.

order.description: Order of the image when display.

delete.summary: Deletes and existing venue image with the specified Id.

parameters.name: venueImageId
parameters.description: The unique Venue Image Id.

get.summary: Retrieves a collection of venue maps.

post.summary: Creates a new Venue Map using the specified request data.

requestBody.description: The details of the Venue Map to create.

get.summary: Retrieves a collection of venue maps from the venue provided.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: Unique venue identifier.

get.summary: Retrieves the venue map details for the specified identifier.
get.description:
This method performs an asynchronous lookup for the venue map. If no venue map is
            found for the given identifier, the response will be 404 Not Found.

parameters.name: venueMapId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the venue map to retrieve. Must be a positive long value.

put.summary: Updates an existing Venue Map with the specified details.

parameters.name: venueMapId
parameters.description: Unique Id of the Venue Map.

requestBody.description: The request object containing the updated Venue Map information.

delete.summary: Deletes the Venue Map with the specified Id.

parameters.name: venueMapId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the Venue Map.

get.summary: Retrieves the venue map layout for the specified identifier.
get.description:
This method performs an asynchronous lookup for the venue map layout. If no venue map layout is
            found for the given identifier, the response will be 404 Not Found.

parameters.name: venueMapId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the venue map layout to retrieve. Must be a positive long value.

get.summary: Retrieves a collection of venue map catalog items for use in selection lists or dropdowns.
get.description:
Use this endpoint to obtain a list of venues formatted for display in UI components
            such as dropdowns. The returned items typically include venue map identifiers and display names.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the venue id of the maps to retrieve. If null then returns all venue maps

get.summary: Retrieves venue map chart data using the unique chart key provided.

parameters.name: chartKey
parameters.description: The unique chart key

get.summary: Retrieves a list of venues asynchronously.
get.description:
This method calls the venue service to obtain the venue list. The response will
            contain the venue data if the operation is successful.

post.summary: Creates a new Venue using the specified request data.

requestBody.description: The details of the Venue to create.

get.summary: Retrieves a venue with the specified identifier.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the venue.

put.summary: Updates an existing Venue with the specified details.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: Unique Id of the Venue.

requestBody.description: The request object containing the updated Venue information.

delete.summary: Deletes the Venue with the specified Id.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the Venue.

get.summary: Retrieves a collection of venue catalog items for use in selection lists or dropdowns.
get.description:
Use this endpoint to obtain a list of venues formatted for display in UI components
            such as dropdowns. The returned items typically include venue identifiers and display names.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of venue cities for use in selection lists or dropdowns.

parameters.name: venueId
parameters.description: The unique Id of the Venue.

parameters.name: venueStatus
parameters.description: The new venue status.
